description: Master catalog of all forged tools

tools.name: TOOL_MAHLER_MEASURE
tools.test_source: Lehmer polynomial (1.17628...), Phi_5 (1.0), golden ratio (1.61803...)
known_issues: Numerical precision degrades above degree ~100 due to root-finding

tools.name: TOOL_GPD_TAIL_FIT
tools.test_source: Synthetic GPD data with known xi = -0.3, 0.0, +0.5 all recovered within 0.03
known_issues: Needs >= 10 exceedances; unstable below 30

tools.name: TOOL_CF_EXPANSION
known_issues: zaremba_test is O(q) — slow for q > 10^6, needs sieve optimization for Tier 2

tools.name: TOOL_SINGULARITY_CLASSIFIER
tools.test_source: Fibonacci (radius 0.6180 correct), Catalan (ALGEBRAIC, alpha~0.5 correct), Constant (ENTIRE correct)
known_issues: Simple poles (B~0) may classify as LOG; partition function needs >50 terms for ESSENTIAL detection

tools.name: TOOL_HYPERBOLIC_VOLUME
known_issues: Volume is 0 for non-hyperbolic knots (torus/satellite) — always pair with is_hyperbolic()
volume_conjecture_ratio slow and often unavailable for high-crossing knots

tools.name: TOOL_ROOT_NUMBER
known_issues: Input is a-invariants; Cremona label lookup requires elldata package (not installed in this env)

tools.name: TOOL_CONDUCTOR
also: global_reduction(ainvs) -> dict (conductor, tamagawa_product, bad_primes, per-prime Kodaira/c_p)
tools.test_source: LMFDB conductors (11a1->11, 37a1->37, 389a1->389, 15a1->15, 5077a1->5077). Tamagawa products cross-checked: 14a1 tam=6=2*3, 27a1 tam=3 at IV*, 11a1 tam=5 at I_5 split.
known_issues: PARI local-data slot convention: c_p at index 3 (not 2 as often documented); this is the source of first-pass bugs

tools.name: TOOL_CLASS_NUMBER
also: class_group(polynomial) -> dict (h, structure, disc, signature, degree)
tools.test_source: Imag quadratic (Q(sqrt(-5))=2, Q(sqrt(-23))=3, Q(sqrt(-47))=5, Q(sqrt(-71))=7, Q(sqrt(-163))=1), real quadratic (Q(sqrt(-79))=3, Q(sqrt(82))=4), cubic (Q(2^(1/3))=1). 32/32 assertions vs Cohen tables + LMFDB nf_fields.
known_issues: For |disc| > 2^50, PARI assumes GRH unless bnfcertify is called explicitly
Polynomial must be irreducible; no validation performed

tools.name: TOOL_REGULATOR
also: mordell_weil(ainvs) -> dict (rank bounds, saturated generators, height matrix, torsion)
tools.test_source: LMFDB ec_curvedata: 37.a1, 43.a1, 53.a1, 58.a1 (rank 1); 389.a1, 433.a1 (rank 2); 5077.a1 (rank 3). Match to 10+ decimals. Rank 0: 11.a1, 14.a1, 15.a1, 27.a1. 16/16 assertions.
known_issues: CRITICAL: PARI ellrank returns independent points but NOT a Z-basis. MUST call ellsaturation (we do); without it regulator is (index)^2 too large. Example: 37.a1 unsaturated gives 0.46 instead of 0.051.
For rank >= 2 at large conductor, ellrank may not prove full rank; use mordell_weil() to inspect rank_lower vs rank_upper.
Default saturation_bound=100; raise for curves with suspected large prime index.

tools.name: TOOL_SMITH_NORMAL_FORM
tools.test_source: Cohen-style example (SNF = diag(2,6,12)), diagonal (2,4,6) -> (2,2,12), triangle boundary map (H_0=Z, H_1=Z, no torsion), Z/4 x Z/6 -> Z/2 x Z/12. 22/22 assertions.
known_issues: sympy backend is O(n^3) but slow in practice: >30x30 matrices take seconds. For large simplicial complexes, promote to Tier 2 with PARI matsnf (12-50x faster in benchmarks).

tools.name: TOOL_GALOIS_GROUP
tools.interface: galois_group(polynomial) -> dict (name, order, transitive_id, parity, is_abelian, degree)
tools.test_source: S2/S3/S4/S5 generic; A3 cyclic cubic (x^3-3x-1); V4 (sqrt(2)+sqrt(3)); D(4) (x^4-2); Frobenius F(5) (x^5-2); cyclotomics Phi_5, Phi_7, Phi_8. 17/17 assertions.
known_issues: PARI polgalois supports degree <= 7 unconditionally; degree 8-11 requires galdata package (not installed). Tool raises ValueError for degree > 11.
Input polynomial must be irreducible over Q; PARI will raise a PariError for reducible input.
is_abelian is heuristic-by-name + order-check; may mislabel rare exotic cases, but order == degree guard catches most.

tools.name: TOOL_LLL_REDUCTION
also: lll_with_transform(basis) -> (R, T) such that R = T @ B
tools.test_source: Skewed basis shortens (norm^2 1e12 -> 1), determinant preservation (5 random lattices), unimodular transform (det T = +/-1, R = T @ B exact), Gram matrix input. 6/6 test functions.
known_issues: CONVENTION: tool accepts rows = basis vectors (natural math convention). PARI uses columns = basis vectors; we transpose internally. Output is also row-convention.
LLL gives short vectors but not shortest; ||b_1|| <= 2^((n-1)/2) * lambda_1. For exact SVP use fpylll (not installed).
Integer lattices only; for rational/real relation finding use mpmath.lindep (PSLQ).

tools.name: TOOL_KNOT_SHAPE_FIELD
tools.test_source: 4_1 -> x^2 - x + 1 disc -3 (Q(sqrt(-3))), 5_2 -> x^3 - x^2 + 1 disc -23 (LMFDB 3.1.23.1). Non-hyperbolic 3_1 correctly raises. 4/4 assertions.
known_issues: CAVEAT: returns SHAPE field, not invariant trace field. K_iTrF <= K_shape <= K_iTrF(i). For most knots they coincide; cross-check with published iTrF tables (Neumann-Reid, Maclachlan-Reid) before using as LMFDB identity-join key.
SnapPy's trace_field_gens().find_field() requires Sage; this is the no-Sage fallback.
Non-hyperbolic knots raise ValueError; check TOOL_HYPERBOLIC_VOLUME.is_hyperbolic() if unsure.

tools.name: TOOL_HILBERT_CLASS_FIELD
tools.interface: hilbert_class_field(polynomial) -> dict (abs_poly, rel_poly, degree_abs, disc, class_number_K, is_trivial)
also: class_field_tower(polynomial, max_depth=5) -> dict (depth, terminates, capped, poly_sequence, class_number_sequence, degree_sequence, disc_sequence)
tools.test_source: Q(sqrt(-5)) h=2 HCF=x^4+3x^2+1 disc=400; Q(i) h=1 trivial; Q(sqrt(-23)) h=3 HCF deg 6; Q(sqrt(-47)) h=5 tower terminates at depth 1. 8/8 assertions.
known_issues: Class field towers may be INFINITE (Golod-Shafarevich). Default max_depth=5 caps iteration; check the 'capped' flag — capped!=False means tower didn't terminate, not 'tower depth > 5'.
bnfinit of deep-tower intermediate fields can be very slow (degree compounds each level). For bulk H15 scans, use class_field_tower(max_depth=1) first to get tower-start behavior.
PARI assumes GRH for |disc| > 2^50. Iterated HCF amplifies GRH dependence.
Variable priority gotcha internally handled (base uses y, HCF uses x).

tools.name: TOOL_ANALYTIC_SHA
tools.interface: analytic_sha(ainvs) -> dict (value, rounded, rank, L_r_over_fact, Omega, Reg, tam, tors, disc_sign)
tools.test_source: LMFDB ec_mwbsd.sha_an: Sha=1 (11.a1 r0, 37.a1 r1, 389.a1 r2, 5077.a1 r3), Sha=4 (66.b1, 102.c1), Sha=9 (182.d1), Sha=16 (210.e1). 12/12 assertions; all round-match exact.
known_issues: r! factor: PARI's ellanalyticrank returns L^(r)(1) RAW, not /r!; tool divides explicitly. Omitting this gives factor r! error (rank 2 -> 2x off, rank 3 -> 6x off).
Real period convention: Omega_E = 2*omega[1] if disc > 0, omega[1] otherwise. PARI's E.omega[1] does NOT include this factor.
Regulator saturation critical: depends on TOOL_REGULATOR which calls ellsaturation(100).
For very large conductor, ellanalyticrank may be slow or hit precision limits. Consider LMFDB values directly.

tools.name: TOOL_SELMER_RANK
also: selmer_2_data(ainvs, effort=1) -> dict (dim_sel_2, rank_lo, rank_hi, rank_proved, sha2_lower, dim_E2)
tools.test_source: rank 0/1/2/3 trivial Sha (11.a1, 37.a1, 389.a1, 5077.a1); 571.b1 (Sha=(Z/2)^2, Sel_2=2); 66.b1 (Sha=(Z/2)^2, 2-tors Z/2, Sel_2=3); 210.e1 (unproved rank from 2-descent, Sel_2=3). 10/10 assertions.
known_issues: Formula subtlety: dim Sel_2 = max(rank_lo+s, rank_hi) + dim E[2](Q). When rank proved, s = dim Sha[2] exactly; else s=0 and rank_hi is the Selmer bound.
Only 2-Selmer; p-Selmer for p > 2 needs Magma or hand-rolled p-descent (not in toolchain).
For large conductor, consider LMFDB precomputed values.

tools.name: TOOL_FALTINGS_HEIGHT
also: faltings_data(ainvs) -> dict (h_F, omega_1, tau, minimal_ainvs, is_minimal)
tools.test_source: LMFDB ec_curvedata.faltings_height: 37.a1 (-0.9965), 389.a1 (-0.7956), 5077.a1 (-0.5614), 66.b1 (-0.0950). Match to 10+ decimals. Non-minimal input test also passes. 6/6 assertions.
known_issues: Formula identity h_F = -log|omega_1| - (1/2) log(Im tau) ONLY holds for global minimal Weierstrass model; tool runs ellminimalmodel first.
For E/Q, LMFDB's faltings_height == stable_faltings_height; formula agrees with both.
Uses double-precision via Python complex; for high precision use mpmath-based periods.
Q-only; NF case needs archimedean sum.

tools.name: TOOL_ALEXANDER_POLYNOMIAL
tools.interface: alexander_polynomial(knot) -> dict (coeffs, coeffs_by_grading, degree, is_unit, determinant)
tools.test_source: knotinfo standard polys: 3_1 [1,-1,1], 4_1 [-1,3,-1], 5_1 [1,-1,1,-1,1], 5_2 [2,-3,2], plus palindrome symmetry check on 6 knots. 11/11 assertions.
known_issues: Knots only, not multi-component links (different convention).
Thin wrapper over knot_floer_homology; the main HFK package already returns the data — tool just does the Euler-characteristic sum + determinant.
No PD code validation; malformed input yields HFK errors.

tools.name: TOOL_FUNCTIONAL_EQ_CHECK
tools.interface: functional_eq_check(obj, precision=100, threshold_log10=-8) -> dict (residual_log10, satisfies, kind, conductor, degree)
tools.test_source: Riemann zeta (residual 10^-61); EC 11.a1, 37.a1, 389.a1, 5077.a1, 66.b1 (residual 10^-60 or better); conductor matches LMFDB; bad-input rejection. 11/11 assertions.
known_issues: lfuncheckfeq returns negative log_10 of residual — positive or near-zero value means FE fails. Default threshold -8 catches outright breakages; raise precision for stringent audits.
Accepts EC ainvs, integer 1 (zeta), PARI expr string, or cypari Gen. No first-class support for modular forms / Hecke characters yet — pass lfuncreate expression string for those.

tools.name: TOOL_TROPICAL_RANK
tools.test_source: Riemann-Roch on K_3 (genus 1): r(deg 2)=1, r(deg 3)=2; path (genus 0): r(D)=deg(D); deg -1 unwinnable. 10/10 assertions.
known_issues: NP-hard in general; practical up to |V| ~ 15.
chipfiring uses multiprocessing — HEREDOC invocation on Windows triggers spawn issues; use script files not stdin-HEREDOC.
Adjacency must be symmetric non-neg integer; disconnected graphs untested.

tools.name: TOOL_CM_ORDER_DATA
tools.interface: cm_order_data(D) -> dict (fundamental_disc, cm_conductor, class_number, is_maximal, ring_class_polynomial, degree)
tools.test_source: 9 Heegner fundamentals (f=1, h=1); 4 non-maximal orders (-12 f=2, -16 f=2, -27 f=3, -28 f=2); Q(sqrt(-15)) h=2. Hilbert class polynomials match knot-info-style reference values. 17/17 assertions.
known_issues: D must be negative and ≡ 0 or 1 mod 4; other inputs raise ValueError.
polclass can be expensive for h > 20; consider class-number cap in bulk scans.
ring_class_polynomial string uses PARI default; wrap in polredabs if canonical matching needed.

tools.name: TOOL_SAT_SOLVER
tools.interface: solve_cnf(clauses: list[list[int]], solver: str = 'kissat', timeout: float | None = None) -> dict
tools.test_source: 3-SAT satisfiable model verification, 3-SAT UNSAT, Caccetta-Haggkvist small certificate n=5/outdegree>=2/no cycles<=3 UNSAT, timeout behavior. 4/4 tests pass.
known_issues: PySAT's bundled kissat404 backend is present, but on this Windows harness it segfaulted on the Caccetta-Haggkvist UNSAT certificate; solver='kissat' records requested_solver='kissat' and uses Glucose3 fallback on Windows.
Timeout returns satisfiable=None with stats.timed_out=True because SAT truth is unknown after interruption.

tools.name: TOOL_TT_SPLICE
tools.interface: tt_splice_compatibility(region_a_npz: Path, region_b_npz: Path, prime_detrend: bool = True, null_perms: int = 300, seed: int = 20260417) -> dict
tools.test_source: Synthetic low-rank shared factor scores high, random Gaussian pair scores low with non-significant null p-value, self-splice scores 1.0, PATTERN_PRIME_GRAVITATIONAL_OVERFIT warning on prime_detrend=False, same seed gives identical bond_ranks. ergon/tensor.npz schema inspected and skipped as full object-feature matrix requiring extracted per-region slices. 5 passed, 1 skipped.
known_issues: Tier 1 implementation uses dense SVD unfoldings, so callers should pass per-region tensor slices rather than full multi-million-row object-feature matrices.
Compatibility score combines TT rank compression with leading-subspace alignment; audit exposes independent ranks, joint ranks, null p-value, and prime-detrend magnitudes for calibration.
When prime_detrend=False the API emits a PATTERN_PRIME_GRAVITATIONAL_OVERFIT warning and records prime_detrend_applied=false.

tools.name: TAIL_VS_BULK_DECOMPOSITION
tools.interface: decompose_spectral(spectral_signal: np.ndarray, tail_threshold: float | str = 'auto', null_model: callable | None = None, n_perms: int = 300, seed: int = 20260417) -> dict
tools.test_source: Synthetic tail-only, bulk-only, tail+bulk, pure-noise, deterministic-seed, and custom battery-contract tests. 6/6 focused tests pass; full Techne suite 134 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning.
known_issues: The tail/bulk threshold is itself a calibration parameter; future work needs calibration against F011's actual spectral structure.
The built-in battery is a placeholder until substrate battery_apply is exposed; callers may provide null_model callable(signal, mask, rng, n_perms) returning effect_size, p_value, and pattern_flags.
Default tail split is coordinate-tail at the 95th percentile, not magnitude-tail.

tools.name: OPERATOR_RANK_PARITY_NULL_CONTROL
tools.interface: rank_parity_null_control(population: np.ndarray | str | list, rank_field: str = 'analytic_rank', conductor_field: str = 'conductor', n_perms: int = 300, seed: int = 20260417) -> dict
tools.test_source: Synthetic {0:60,1:30,2:10} marginal preservation, two-population rank-distribution difference, F011-shaped non-CM/CM/G2C rank-parity asymmetry, determinism, single-rank trivial null, underdetermined conductor decile flag, and NPZ path input. 7/7 focused tests pass; full Techne suite 141 passed, 1 skipped, 1 warning.
known_issues: This operator does not run the F011 audit; it only provides the joint-marginal-preserving null needed by Track A.
Pairwise population comparison currently reports L1 distances over rank and parity distributions; downstream audits must choose claim-specific statistics.
Conductor deciles degrade gracefully for small or low-unique-conductor populations and set conductor_decile_audit.underdetermined=true.

stats.notes: Cycle 33: OPERATOR_RANK_PARITY_NULL_CONTROL shipped (REQ-030). Extends NULL_BSWCD@v2 to preserve (conductor decile, rank parity) joint marginals and reports multi-population rank-parity asymmetry.
